From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

1979 Italian crime comedy film

| The Gang That Sold America | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Directed by | [Bruno Corbucci](/wiki/Bruno_Corbucci "Bruno Corbucci") |
| Screenplay by | * [Mario Amendola](/wiki/Mario_Amendola "Mario Amendola") * Bruno Corbucci[[1]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2013259-1) |
| Story by | * Mario Amendola * Bruno Corbucci[[1]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2013259-1) |
| Produced by | [Galliano Juso](/w/index.php?title=Galliano_Juso&action=edit&redlink=1 "Galliano Juso (page does not exist)")[[1]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2013259-1) |
| Starring | * [Tomas Milian](/wiki/Tomas_Milian "Tomas Milian") * [Enzo Cannavale](/wiki/Enzo_Cannavale "Enzo Cannavale") * [Margherita Fumero](/wiki/Margherita_Fumero "Margherita Fumero") * [Gianni Musy](/wiki/Gianni_Musy "Gianni Musy") |

***The Gang That Sold America*** ([Italian](/wiki/Italian_language "Italian language"): ***Squadra antigangsters***) is a 1979 Italian "[poliziottesco](/wiki/Poliziotteschi "Poliziotteschi")"-[comedy film](/wiki/Comedy_film "Comedy film") directed by [Bruno Corbucci](/wiki/Bruno_Corbucci "Bruno Corbucci"). It is the fifth chapter in the Nico Giraldi film series starred by [Tomas Milian](/wiki/Tomas_Milian "Tomas Milian").[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-ccc-3) The Italian progressive rock band [Goblin](/wiki/Goblin_(band) "Goblin (band)") created the soundtrack for the film.[[1]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2013259-1)

## Cast

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Gang_That_Sold_America&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Cast")]

* [Tomas Milian](/wiki/Tomas_Milian "Tomas Milian") as Nico Giraldi
* [Enzo Cannavale](/wiki/Enzo_Cannavale "Enzo Cannavale") as Salvatore Esposito
* [Asha Puthli](/wiki/Asha_Puthli "Asha Puthli") as Fiona Strike
* Leo Gavero as Don Vito
* [Isa Danieli](/wiki/Isa_Danieli "Isa Danieli") as Salvatore Esposito's Wife
* [Margherita Fumero](/wiki/Margherita_Fumero "Margherita Fumero") as Maria Sole Giarra
* [Gianni Musy](/wiki/Gianni_Musy "Gianni Musy") as Gitto Cardone
* Tomas Milian Jr. as Antonio, Salvatore Esposito's Child
* [Andrea Aureli](/wiki/Andrea_Aureli "Andrea Aureli") as Don Mimì
* [Irwin Keyes](/wiki/Irwin_Keyes "Irwin Keyes") as Support Killer

## Production

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Gang_That_Sold_America&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Production")]

*The Gang That Sold America* is one of the 11 films in which the Cuban actor [Tomas Milian](/wiki/Tomas_Milian "Tomas Milian") portrayed the character of [Commissioner](/wiki/Police_commissioner "Police commissioner") Nico Giraldi between 1976 and 1984.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Release

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Gang_That_Sold_America&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Release")]

*The Gang that Sold America* was released on March 1, 1979 where it was distributed by [Titanus](/wiki/Titanus "Titanus").[[1]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2013259-1) It grossed a total of 569 million Italian lire.[[1]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECurti2013259-1)
